Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.guava
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.guava

Interface GuavaRequirements

All Superinterfaces:
Configuration
All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultGuavaRequirements
public interface GuavaRequirements
extends Configuration
Verifies the requirements types from of the Guava library API.

Implementations must be thread-safe.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    <K,​ V> MultimapVerifier<K,​V> assertThat​(Multimap<K,​V> actual, String name)
    Same as requireThat(Multimap, String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
    GuavaRequirements putContext​(String name, Object value)
    Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message.
    GuavaRequirements removeContext​(String name)
    Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
    <K,​ V> MultimapVerifier<K,​V> requireThat​(Multimap<K,​V> actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a Multimap.
    <K,​ V> MultimapValidator<K,​V> validateThat​(Multimap<K,​V> actual, String name)
    Verifies the requirements of a Multimap.
    GuavaRequirements withAssertionsDisabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
    GuavaRequirements withAssertionsEnabled()
    Indicates that assertThat() should invoke requireThat().
    GuavaRequirements withCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
    GuavaRequirements withConfiguration​(Configuration configuration)
    Replaces a verifier's configuration.
    GuavaRequirements withDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    GuavaRequirements withoutCleanStackTrace()
    Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
    GuavaRequirements withoutDiff()
    Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
    <T> GuavaRequirements withoutStringConverter​(Class<T> type)
    Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
    <T> GuavaRequirements withStringConverter​(Class<T> type, Function<T,​String> converter)
    Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

    Methods declared in interface com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.Configuration

    assertionsAreEnabled, getContext, isCleanStackTrace, isDiffEnabled, toString

  • Method Details

    • putContext

      GuavaRequirements putContext​(String name, Object value)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Adds or updates contextual information associated with the exception message. Overrides any values associated with the name at the ThreadRequirements level.
      Specified by:
      putContext in interface Configuration
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      value - the value of the parameter
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • removeContext

      GuavaRequirements removeContext​(String name)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Removes contextual information associated with the exception message.
      Specified by:
      removeContext in interface Configuration
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the parameter
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withAssertionsDisabled

      GuavaRequirements withAssertionsDisabled()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that assertThat() shouldn't do anything.
      Specified by:
      withAssertionsDisabled in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withAssertionsEnabled

      GuavaRequirements withAssertionsEnabled()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that assertThat() should invoke requireThat().
      Specified by:
      withAssertionsEnabled in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withCleanStackTrace

      GuavaRequirements withCleanStackTrace()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exception stack traces should omit references to this library.
      Specified by:
      withCleanStackTrace in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      See Also:
      Configuration.isCleanStackTrace()

    • withoutCleanStackTrace

      GuavaRequirements withoutCleanStackTrace()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exception stack traces should contain references to this library.
      Specified by:
      withoutCleanStackTrace in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration
      See Also:
      Configuration.isCleanStackTrace()

    • withDiff

      GuavaRequirements withDiff()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exceptions should show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Specified by:
      withDiff in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withoutDiff

      GuavaRequirements withoutDiff()
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that exceptions should not show the difference between the actual and expected values.
      Specified by:
      withoutDiff in interface Configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withStringConverter

      <T> GuavaRequirements withStringConverter​(Class<T> type, Function<T,​String> converter)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that a function should be used to convert an object to a String.

      Please note that type must be an exact match (e.g. configuring a converter for Set will not match values of type HashSet).

      Specified by:
      withStringConverter in interface Configuration
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted (non-primitive arrays are mapped to Object[].class)
      converter - a function that converts an object of the specified type to a String
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withoutStringConverter

      <T> GuavaRequirements withoutStringConverter​(Class<T> type)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Indicates that an object's toString() method should be used to convert it to a String.
      Specified by:
      withoutStringConverter in interface Configuration
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of object being converted
      Parameters:
      type - the type of object being converted
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • withConfiguration

      GuavaRequirements withConfiguration​(Configuration configuration)
      Description copied from interface: Configuration
      Replaces a verifier's configuration.
      Specified by:
      withConfiguration in interface Configuration
      Parameters:
      configuration - a new configuration
      Returns:
      the updated configuration

    • requireThat

      <K,​ V> MultimapVerifier<K,​V> requireThat​(Multimap<K,​V> actual, String name)
      Verifies the requirements of a Multimap.
      Type Parameters:
      K - the type of key in the multimap
      V - the type of value in the multimap
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a verifier for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • assertThat

      <K,​ V> MultimapVerifier<K,​V> assertThat​(Multimap<K,​V> actual, String name)
      Same as requireThat(Multimap, String) but does nothing if assertions are disabled.
      Type Parameters:
      K - the type of key in the multimap
      V - the type of value in the multimap
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a verifier for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty

    • validateThat

      <K,​ V> MultimapValidator<K,​V> validateThat​(Multimap<K,​V> actual, String name)
      Verifies the requirements of a Multimap.
      Type Parameters:
      K - the type of key in the multimap
      V - the type of value in the multimap
      Parameters:
      actual - the actual value
      name - the name of the value
      Returns:
      a validator for the value
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is empty
